document_identity.principal_address: 24 Frank Lloyd Wright Drive, Ann Arbor, Michigan 48105-9484

item1_entity_structure.franchisor: Domino's Pizza Franchising LLC (Delaware LLC, organized March 2, 2007)
parent_chain: Domino's Pizza Master Issuer LLC (DPMI, Delaware LLC)
Domino's Pizza, Inc. (DPI, Delaware corp, publicly traded)

key_affiliates.name: Domino's IP Holder LLC
key_affiliates.role: Owns all U.S. trademarks; 99-year license to DPF

key_affiliates.name: Domino's Pizza Distribution LLC (DPD)
key_affiliates.role: Supply chain; sells food/supplies/software to franchisees; 2024 franchisee revenue $2.6B

key_affiliates.name: DP International Franchising
key_affiliates.role: International franchisor; 14,352 international franchises as of end-2024

item1_entity_structure.securitization: 2007 securitization transaction created DPF as franchisor; DPL provides services under management agreement

pending_actions.case: State of Rhode Island v. Domino's Pizza, Inc. et al.
pending_actions.allegation: Misleading statements about global net store growth
pending_actions.status: Amended complaint filed April 2025; defendants intend to defend

pending_actions.allegation: Breach of fiduciary duties, insider trading (follow-on from securities action)
pending_actions.status: All stayed pending motion to dismiss in securities action

pending_actions.allegation: Franchisee employees alleging DPL is joint employer

concluded_actions.case: DP International v. Van Spaandonk (South Africa master franchise dispute)

item6_royalty.royalty_sales_definition: Total receipts from all sales excluding taxes, coupons, and similar discounts; stadium/airport commissions deducted for Non-Traditional

item6_rent.note: Franchisee leases own premises; 3 months rent estimate $3,000-$25,000; generally 5-year leases with 5-year renewal option

item8_supplier_restrictions.profit_sharing_plan: Optional 10-year DPD profit-sharing plan for franchisees

item9_obligations.key_sections: Franchise Agreement Sections 2-22, Development Agreement Sections 1-16

item11_training.prerequisite: 12 consecutive months as store GM (single store) or supervisor (multi-unit)
item11_training.training_director: Ella Meier, Director of Training & Activation (since June 2013)

traditional.area_of_primary_responsibility: ~1 mile radius (1/2 mile in dense areas)
traditional.delivery_area: Defined by franchisor, adjustable at sole discretion
traditional.overlap_protection: No store with overlapping area of primary responsibility during FA term
traditional.excluded: Enclosed malls, stadiums, airports, theme parks, convention centers

item12_territory.different_brand_rights: Franchisor may establish outlets under different brands in your area

item14_patents.copyright_protection: Operating manuals, training materials, software, advertising materials

item15_participation.no_outside_business: Without written approval (may be withheld in sole judgment)

item17_renewal.materially_different_quote: You are required to sign our then current form of franchise agreement which may contain other terms materially different from the terms of your original franchise agreement

item17_termination.by_franchisee: If DPF breaches and doesn't cure after 30-day written notice

item17_noncompete.during_term: No involvement in any similar business, anywhere
item17_noncompete.post_term_geographic: At Store premises or within 10 miles

item17_dispute_resolution.choice_of_law: Laws of state where Store is located

item17_death_disability.proposal_deadline: 120 days after death or permanent disability

item18_public_figures.statement: We do not use any public figure to promote our franchise.

item19_financial_performance.siting_model: SIMMS (Domino's Strategic Integrated Mapping and Modeling System)

item20_outlet_data.gag_clause_quote: We have not entered into agreements with confidentiality clauses that would prevent franchisees from discussing their experience with Domino's

item22_contracts_listed: Exhibit E — Traditional Store Standard Franchise Agreement
Exhibit F — Non-Traditional Store Franchise Agreement
Exhibit H — Domino's Pizza Help Desk Services Agreement
Exhibit I — Sale of Assets Agreement
Exhibit J — Domino's PULSE Software License Agreement
Exhibit O — Domino's Pizza Online Ordering Franchisee Services Agreement
Exhibit Q — Global Positioning System Technology Platform Agreement
Exhibit R — Domino's Rider to Services Agreement

special_risks.risk: No state-specific special risks disclosed on Special Risks page
special_risks.note: Page directs reader to check State Specific Addenda

overrides.override_summary: Fast Food Act compliance requirements added to Items 1, 5, 6, 7, 11 — $20/hour minimum wage for fast food workers effective April 2024 with annual increases up to 3.5%
Claims under Illinois Franchise Law excepted from any choice of forum/law clause
Standard Hawaii franchise investment law disclosure language
All initial fees deferred until franchisor completes pre-opening obligations; development fees deferred until first franchise opens
Release at renewal/transfer does not apply to MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law liability
Termination upon insolvency may not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law
Claims under MD law must be brought within 3 years after grant of franchise
Cannot require litigation outside MN, waiver of jury trial, liquidated damages, termination penalties, or judgment notes
90 days notice of termination (60 days to cure); 180 days notice for non-renewal
Franchisor will not unreasonably withhold consent if transferee meets standards
Release at renewal/transfer not applicable to extent prohibited by MN Rule 2860.4400D
Service charges capped at $30 per Minn. Stat. 604.113
Washington Franchise Investment Protection Act prevails in conflicts of law
Arbitration/mediation must be in WA or mutually agreed location; franchisee may bring WA franchise law action in WA
Release cannot include WA Act rights except in negotiated settlement with independent counsel
Transfer fees collectable only to extent reflecting reasonable actual/estimated costs
Noncompete void against employees earning <$100K/year and contractors earning <$250K/year (adjusted for inflation)
Franchisor cannot restrict franchisee from soliciting/hiring employees of other franchisees or franchisor; AOD with WA Attorney General (Oct 2018)

unresolveds.description: Active securities class action and derivative lawsuits against DPI officers (Weiner, Reddy, Jordan, D'Elia) — outcome could affect franchise system
DPL servicer replacement mechanism under securitization untested — if DPL fails, franchise services depend on backup arrangements
Brand Technology modification cap (1.5% of cumulative Royalty Sales) excludes initial PULSE acquisition and renewal — effective tech cost ceiling unclear
Insurance cost estimate ($25K-$75K Traditional) may be 'significantly higher' depending on state/location/loss history — no disclosed maximum
DPL member's deficit of $(3.95)B and total debt ~$5B — financial stress at DPL could impact service delivery to franchisees
